Add late warmdown STE QAT for the exact mixed int5/int6 export path (MLP int5; attention/bigram int6).
reasoning: Diagnosis: All 18 prior iterations crashed at the API layer, so there is no model-side evidence against a substantive change. In the current script, the most likely remaining bottleneck is train/eval mismatch: optimization happens in float/bf16, but the final score is taken after 3% pruning plus aggressive mix | Hypothesis: Add late-stage quantization-aware training with a straight-through estimator on exactly the tensors that are exported as int5/int6: MLP weights at int5, attention/bigram weights at int6, while leaving fp16-kept tensors alone. Enabling it only once during warmdown should let the model adapt to rowwis | Expected: -0.0055 bpb. This is the highest-confidence path because the current SOTA script is already heavily compression-constrained, so recovering post-quantization loss is more plausible than adding capacity; late STE QAT commonly recovers roughly half of the PTQ gap on models this small, which should be in the ~0.004 to ~0.007 bpb range here. | CRASH: TIMEOUT: Experiment exceeded 25-minute hard limit (10 train + 10 eval + 5 buffer)

description: Add late exact-path STE QAT for the mixed int5/int6 export scheme.
reasoning: Diagnosis: All 19 prior iterations provide no model-side negative evidence: 18 died at the API layer and the last one crashed before a completed benchmark. In this script, the most likely bottleneck is train/export mismatch: optimization happens in float, but the scored artifact is after 3% pruning plus exact  | Hypothesis: Add late quantization-aware training that matches the exact export path: per-row int5 for MLP weights, per-row int6 for attention and bigram weights, while respecting the existing FP16-kept exceptions. Ramping this in over the last half of warmdown should let weights migrate onto quantization-friend | Expected: -0.006 bpb; exact-path late QAT on an aggressive mixed int5/int6 export usually recovers roughly 0.003-0.008 bpb on models of this scale, and this script currently evaluates the dequantized export rather than the float model. | CRASH: TIMEOUT: Experiment exceeded 25-minute hard limit (10 train + 10 eval + 5 buffer)
